commit.message:
feat: GitHub Actions AI maintainer workflow

Replaces the non-functional placeholder workflow with a real agentic
implementation that processes 'ai-task' issues automatically.

.github/workflows/ai-maintainer.yml
- Triggers on issues: [labeled] (not a cron schedule)
- Filters for label == 'ai-task' in the job condition
- Supports workflow_dispatch for manual re-processing
- Concurrency group prevents parallel runs on the same issue
- Installs zbx + openai, then runs ai_maintainer.py

.github/scripts/ai_maintainer.py
- Reads issue context from env vars (injected by the workflow)
- Calls GitHub Models API (gpt-4o, OpenAI-compatible via GITHUB_TOKEN)
  at https://models.inference.ai.azure.com
- Agentic loop (max 30 rounds) with 5 tools:
    read_file     — read any repo file
    list_files    — list a directory
    write_file    — create or overwrite a file
    validate      — run zbx validate <path> for YAML correctness
    finish        — signal completion with branch/PR title/body
- After finish(): creates branch, commits, pushes, opens PR via gh CLI
- Posts a comment on the issue with the PR link (or skip reason)
- Skips unclear/risky issues gracefully (SKIP: prefix convention)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(kernels): prevent f32 overflow in NEON RMSNorm proptests (#3327)

## What changed
Added \prop_assume!(input.iter().all(|&x| x.abs() < 1e18))\ to all four
\prop_neon_rmsnorm_*\ tests.

## Why
\prop::num::f32::NORMAL\ generates values up to ~3.4e38. Squaring values
> ~1e18 overflows f32 (max ~3.4e38), producing infinite RMS and
zero-valued outputs.

**Failing input** (from CI): \[6.19e22, 1.17e-13]\
- \(6.19e22) = 3.83e45\  overflows f32  \sum = inf\  \ms = inf\
- \x / inf = 0\  output RMS = 0, test expects ~1.0

**Affected tests:**
- \prop_neon_rmsnorm_unit_rms\ (confirmed failure on macOS ARM64 CI)
- \prop_neon_rmsnorm_preserves_sign\ (vulnerable: sign of 0.0 sign of
input)
- \prop_neon_rmsnorm_gamma_scaling\ (vulnerable: both outputs would be
0)
- \prop_neon_rmsnorm_matches_scalar\ (probably safe: scalar overflows
identically)

The bound \1e18\ ensures \x * max_n < f32::MAX\ (\(1e18) 256 = 2.56e38 <
3.4e38\).

## What I ran
- Analyzed the failing CI log from \
eon_property_tests\ on macOS ARM64
- Verified all LayerNorm tests already use bounded ranges
(\-10.0..10.0\, \-1e6..1e6\)
- RoPE tests use deterministic data (not random floats)

## Parity evidence
N/A  test filter only, no kernel changes

## Docs touched
None

## What remains
- PR #3323 (Apple Silicon \half\ dep fix) macOS ARM64 failure was caused
by this flake
- After merging, #3323 should pass macOS ARM64 on rebase/re-trigger

## Recommended disposition
**A) MERGE NOW** 6-line \prop_assume!\ addition, same pattern as
batch_norm fix (#3322)

commit.message:
feat: checks framework for contributor-friendly monitoring

Introduces configs/checks/ as the standard home for self-contained
monitoring checks. A check is a folder with a script + check.yaml
that bundles the Zabbix template definition and agent deployment
config in a single file — no other files need to change.

New: configs/checks/ folder convention
- configs/checks/CONTRIBUTING.md — step-by-step contributor guide
- configs/checks/s3-monitoring/check.yaml — reference check (migrated
  from configs/s3-monitoring/); includes template + agent block
- configs/checks/s3-monitoring/README.md — check-level documentation

New: zbx scaffold <name>
- Generates configs/checks/<name>/ with skeleton check.yaml, placeholder
  script and README in one command
- Prints next-steps instructions after scaffolding

New: --from-check option on agent subcommands
- zbx agent diff   <host> --from-check configs/checks/<name>/
- zbx agent deploy <host> --from-check configs/checks/<name>/
- zbx agent test   <host> --from-check configs/checks/<name>/
  Reads the agent: block from check.yaml and merges it into the host's
  existing agent config (deduplicates by dest path / remote_path)

Model: Template.agent (Optional[AgentConfig])
- check.yaml files may include an agent: block in the same format as
  inventory.yaml; ignored by zbx apply, consumed by --from-check

Contributor workflow (no inventory.yaml changes needed):
  zbx scaffold my-check
  # edit configs/checks/my-check/{check.yaml,my_check.py}
  zbx validate configs/checks/my-check/
  zbx apply    configs/checks/my-check/
  zbx agent deploy <host> --from-check configs/checks/my-check/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add visit budget checks to IsMonotonicallyIncreasing and ComputeDoesOverflow (#125156)

`IsMonotonicallyIncreasing` and `ComputeDoesOverflow` lacked the
`m_nVisitBudget` guard that `ComputeRange` already uses, allowing
unbounded recursion cost in pathological cases.

## Description

Added early-exit budget checks to both functions, consistent with the
existing pattern in `ComputeRange`:

```cpp
if (IsOverBudget())
{
    return false;
}
m_nVisitBudget--;
```

- **`IsMonotonicallyIncreasing`**: `false` is the conservative result
("not proven monotonic"), so this is safe to return when over budget.
- **`ComputeDoesOverflow`**: `false` ("doesn't overflow") is slightly
optimistic in isolation, but `ComputeRange` independently returns
`Unknown` when over budget, which already blocks bounds-check
elimination — so no unsound optimization can occur.

Both checks are placed **before** `GetSearchPath()->Set(...)` to avoid
leaving stale entries in the search path on early exit.

perf(inference): eliminate second Vec allocation in softmax hot path (#3324)

## What changed
Replace \.map().collect()\ with in-place division for the normalization
step in all three softmax implementations:
- \decode_strategy.rs\
- \dense_generation.rs\
- \sampling_utils.rs\

Each softmax call now allocates **one Vec instead of two**. For a
32K-vocab model, this eliminates ~128KB of allocation churn per token
generation step.

## What I ran locally
\\\ ash
cargo test -p bitnet-inference --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- softmax --nocapture
# 6 passed; 0 failed
\\\

## CI jobs relied on
CI Core (PR CI run)

## Parity evidence
N/A  no kernel changes

## Perf receipt
Not claiming measured perf improvement  this is allocation hygiene.
The theoretical improvement is 1 fewer Vec<f32> allocation (vocab-size)
per token.

## Docs touched
None

## What remains / risks
- \ op_k_filter\ and \ op_p_filter\ still allocate per-token (separate
PR)
- \dense_generation::softmax_into()\ already exists as a zero-alloc
variant but isn't used everywhere

## Recommended disposition
A) MERGE NOW

commit.message:
v0.1.36: fix is_routable_addr, log disconnect causes, increase idle timeout

- is_routable_addr: filter ALL private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x.x)
  Previously only filtered virtual bridge ranges, so 192.168.x.x passed through
  causing relay circuits to target private server LAN IPs instead of public IP
- ConnectionClosed: log cause field on client for debugging disconnects
- Add OutgoingConnectionError + IncomingConnectionError handlers to client
  (previously silently swallowed, making 4G dial failures invisible)
- Identify::Received: fall back to known_game_peers addr for relay_base when
  server only advertises private IPs in identify (e.g. no external_addr set)
- Increase server and relay idle_connection_timeout from 60s to 3600s
  Prevents libp2p from closing established connections before gossipsub meshes
